commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer routing and the broad doctor/gateway contract choices need human owner review rather than an automated repair branch.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the TypeScript doctor and test diff; the remaining concern is operational service-lifetime behavior, not a security boundary change. (sha=9c35ac54686c0e3c4448208f3f448235e6ae2af6)
commands.review_summary:
The branch converts selected `openclaw doctor` interactive maintenance repairs to finding-scoped structured health-check repairs, adds doctor context fields for prompts, notes, environment, dry-run, and diff plumbing, and expands focused doctor repair tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a structured doctor repair-contract migration rather than a reported bug. The behavior is reviewable through the PR diff, focused tests, and posted CLI/log proof for converted repair paths.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer routing and the broad doctor/gateway contract choices need human owner review rather than an automated repair branch.

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Goal

Start the repair-side migration for structured `openclaw doctor` checks with a small set of interactive maintenance repairs.

The structured health-check contract already gives doctor a read-only detection shape:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> HealthRepairResult
```

This PR is where the repair half starts doing real work. It applies that shape to repairs that prompt, mutate state, report effects, support dry-run previews, and need post-repair validation. The walkthroughs here tighten the contract future repair conversions should follow: `detect()` remains the read-only source of truth; `repair()` receives the exact findings it may fix; repair output is machine-useful through `changes`, `warnings`, `diffs`, and `effects`.

The broader repair wave uses that same output shape so doctor can eventually show dry-run plans, diffs, and effects consistently for repairable findings. This base PR does not turn that richer reporting into the default user surface by itself; the follow-on chain at the end converts the remaining repair buckets first so the feature can be enabled once the migrated majority has the same contract.

Notes are threaded through like the rest of OpenClaw: presentation only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, no runner note arrays.

## Legacy To Modern Shape

Legacy interactive doctor code decided what to find, what to ask, what to mutate, and what to print inside local flows. This PR starts moving that behavior into an isomorphic health-check shape: the same `detect()` result can power lint, repair selection, dry-run reporting, and post-repair validation. The repair path remains interactive where it already was interactive, but the output is now structured enough for later preview and automation surfaces.

## Converted Here

This PR migrates the first interactive maintenance checks into structured doctor repairs:

- MCP invalid entry removal
- MCP stale config repair
- MCP safe config cleanup
- MCP API migration
- legacy project migration
- ambiguous agent path detection
- invalid agent metadata detection
- orphan agent cleanup

These are useful base cases because they cover prompts, filesystem mutation, dry-run previews, and follow-up validation without reaching into plugin-specific or provider-specific policy.

## Review Surface

This is the contract slice to challenge before the rest of the stack builds on it:

- whether `repair(ctx, findings)` is the right repair boundary for converted doctor checks
- whether detect-after validation should run after every successful non-dry-run repair
- whether `changes`, `warnings`, `effects`, and `diffs` are the right machine-readable repair vocabulary
- whether notes should stay purely presentation-only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`

## Follow-On Stack

This is the base PR for the current doctor repair stack:

| PR | Area |
| --- | --- |
| #84290 | plugin registry repairs |
| #84326 | sandbox repairs |
| #84340 | gateway service repairs |
| #84366 | session state repairs |
| #84450 | cron/config audit repairs |
| #84472 | dry-run preview reports |

Those PRs should build on this contract instead of reintroducing per-flow repair notes, ad hoc lint output, or local presentation models.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- Existing CI should provide broader validation for the full stack after the refreshed single-commit PRs are replayed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR starts the structured Doctor repair migration by applying finding-scoped repair, machine-readable repair results, presentation-only notes through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, and detect-after validation to the first interactive maintenance repairs.

Real environment tested: Windows checkout at commit `9c35ac54686c0e3c4448208f3f448235e6ae2af6`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: 5 focused Doctor test files passed, 55 tests total, covering structured repair execution, repair filtering, positional repair ordering, dry-run behavior, diff propagation, presentation-only notes, and detect-after repair validation.

Observed result after fix: The converted repair paths expose findings and repair outputs only. Human notes flow through the existing suppressible note system when a doctor runner provides `ctx.doctor.note(...)`. Lint remains findings-only. Positional core repairs do not re-register bundled health checks while moving through the Doctor contribution order.

What was not tested: Full doctor-stack CI and platform-wide doctor scenarios were not rerun locally for this body refresh; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.
target.author: giodl73-repo

target.body:
## Goal

Start the repair-side migration for structured `openclaw doctor` checks with a small set of interactive maintenance repairs.

The structured health-check contract already gives doctor a read-only detection shape:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> HealthRepairResult
```

This PR is where the repair half starts doing real work. It applies that shape to repairs that prompt, mutate state, report effects, support dry-run previews, and need post-repair validation. The walkthroughs here tighten the contract future repair conversions should follow: `detect()` remains the read-only source of truth; `repair()` receives the exact findings it may fix; repair output is machine-useful through `changes`, `warnings`, `diffs`, and `effects`.

The broader repair wave uses that same output shape so doctor can eventually show dry-run plans, diffs, and effects consistently for repairable findings. This base PR does not turn that richer reporting into the default user surface by itself; the follow-on chain at the end converts the remaining repair buckets first so the feature can be enabled once the migrated majority has the same contract.

Notes are threaded through like the rest of OpenClaw: presentation only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, no runner note arrays.

## Legacy To Modern Shape

Legacy interactive doctor code decided what to find, what to ask, what to mutate, and what to print inside local flows. This PR starts moving that behavior into an isomorphic health-check shape: the same `detect()` result can power lint, repair selection, dry-run reporting, and post-repair validation. The repair path remains interactive where it already was interactive, but the output is now structured enough for later preview and automation surfaces.

## Converted Here

This PR migrates the first interactive maintenance checks into structured doctor repairs:

- MCP invalid entry removal
- MCP stale config repair
- MCP safe config cleanup
- MCP API migration
- legacy project migration
- ambiguous agent path detection
- invalid agent metadata detection
- orphan agent cleanup

These are useful base cases because they cover prompts, filesystem mutation, dry-run previews, and follow-up validation without reaching into plugin-specific or provider-specific policy.

## Review Surface

This is the contract slice to challenge before the rest of the stack builds on it:

- whether `repair(ctx, findings)` is the right repair boundary for converted doctor checks
- whether detect-after validation should run after every successful non-dry-run repair
- whether `changes`, `warnings`, `effects`, and `diffs` are the right machine-readable repair vocabulary
- whether notes should stay purely presentation-only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`

## Follow-On Stack

This is the base PR for the current doctor repair stack:

| PR | Area |
| --- | --- |
| #84290 | plugin registry repairs |
| #84326 | sandbox repairs |
| #84340 | gateway service repairs |
| #84366 | session state repairs |
| #84450 | cron/config audit repairs |
| #84472 | dry-run preview reports |

Those PRs should build on this contract instead of reintroducing per-flow repair notes, ad hoc lint output, or local presentation models.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`
- Existing CI should provide broader validation for the full stack after the refreshed single-commit PRs are replayed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: This PR starts the structured Doctor repair migration by applying finding-scoped repair, machine-readable repair results, presentation-only notes through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`, and detect-after validation to the first interactive maintenance repairs.

Real environment tested: Windows checkout at commit `9c35ac54686c0e3c4448208f3f448235e6ae2af6`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: 5 focused Doctor test files passed, 55 tests total, covering structured repair execution, repair filtering, positional repair ordering, dry-run behavior, diff propagation, presentation-only notes, and detect-after repair validation.

Observed result after fix: The converted repair paths expose findings and repair outputs only. Human notes flow through the existing suppressible note system when a doctor runner provides `ctx.doctor.note(...)`. Lint remains findings-only. Positional core repairs do not re-register bundled health checks while moving through the Doctor contribution order.

What was not tested: Full doctor-stack CI and platform-wide doctor scenarios were not rerun locally for this body refresh; those should run through normal PR CI across the stack.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
